title: Introducing Oak: The AI-Native Identity Operating System | Greylock
gist: Greylock argues legacy identity governance (IGA/IAM) is broken because machine and AI-agent identities now outnumber humans 100-to-1, and backs Oak, an AI-native platform consolidating identity control into a single plane, with $60M in seed funding co-led by Greylock, Accel, and CRV.

insights.claim: Legacy identity governance tooling (IGA, IAM) was designed for a static world of human users and periodic certification, a model that has been broken by the rise of machine and AI-agent identities.
insights.speaker: Mor Chen
insights.so_what: Incumbent identity governance vendors face architectural obsolescence, opening displacement opportunity for AI-native challengers.
insights.quote: Legacy tooling built to govern it (IGA, IAM) was designed for a static world of human users and periodic certification.
insights.locator: Identity has become the largest unresolved

insights.claim: Machine and AI-agent identities now outnumber human identities by more than 100 to 1, a ratio that is compounding as agentic AI spreads through enterprises.
insights.speaker: Mor Chen
insights.so_what: The non-human identity attack surface is exploding, making machine-identity governance a fast-growing security spend category to watch.
insights.quote: machine and AI-agent identities now vastly outnumber human ones by more than 100 to 1, and that ratio is compounding

insights.claim: AI now makes it possible to operate on identity data at a coverage and speed that manual, rules-based systems cannot approach, opening a window to consolidate a fragmented multi-billion-dollar market around a single platform.
insights.speaker: Mor Chen
insights.so_what: The consolidation thesis implies winner-take-most dynamics in identity, favoring an early platform bet over point solutions.
insights.quote: this has opened a rare window to consolidate a fragmented, multi-billion-dollar market around a single platform
insights.locator: Second, AI has made it possible

themes.theme: The Oak bet: product and team

insights.claim: Oak is building an AI-native platform that replaces the fragmented stack of legacy identity governance tools with a single, continuously updated control plane governing every human, machine, and AI-agent identity.
insights.speaker: Mor Chen
insights.so_what: A unified identity control plane is the productization of the consolidation thesis; watch whether it displaces multiple incumbent tools per customer.
insights.quote: a single, continuously updated control plane: one that governs every identity across an organization, whether human, machine, or AI agent
insights.locator: Oak is building an AI-native platform

insights.claim: Oak's founding team is led by Shai Morag, a repeat founder who has already built and sold three security companies, joined by co-founder and CPO Tal Marom, who led product teams at Tenable and Salesforce.
insights.speaker: Mor Chen
insights.so_what: A serial security-exit founder plus proven product leadership is the primary de-risking signal in an early-stage, competitive category.
insights.quote: he brought the urgency and hunger of someone with everything still to prove, paired with hard-won experience that comes from having already built and sold three security companies
insights.locator: The Oak founding team has worked

insights.claim: Oak emerged from stealth with $60 million in seed financing co-led by Greylock, Accel, and CRV.
insights.speaker: Mor Chen
insights.so_what: An unusually large seed with three top-tier co-leads signals high conviction and heavy capitalization for a land-grab in identity security.
insights.quote: Oak emerges from stealth today with $60 million in seed financing co-led by Greylock, Accel, and CRV.
